original: Arsacius of Tarsus is installed as puppet Archbishop of Constantinople after the expulsion of  John Chrysostom, prompting people of the city to worship outside the city walls in protest
St Agatho begins his reign as Catholic Pope
Battle of Conquereuil won by the Count of Anjou, Fulk the Black, defeating Conan I, Count of Brittany, in western France
Ladislaus I, Árpád dynasty King (1077-95) venerated as Roman Catholic saint
Republic of Dubrovnik is founded
Cathedral Church of Charcas established in Charcas, Spanish Empire (later La Plata, now Sucre, Bolivia)
Duke of Alva's army occupies Portugal
Disastrous Dutch expedition to find new spice routes and harass Spanish territories leaves Rotterdam, only one of the five ships with just 36 crew ever return
New Amsterdam (now New York City) enacts first speed limit law in North America
First Sea Battle of Lagos: French fleet under Anne Hilarion de Tourville defeats an Anglo-Dutch fleet under George Rooke
Polish parliament selects monarch August of Saxony as king
French fleet conquer St Philips Castle of Minorca
Buenos Aires captured by British
Prudence Crandall, a white woman, arrested for conducting an academy for black females at Canterbury, Connecticut
New York and Boston are linked by telegraph wires
Bibighar massacre of 120 British women and children following the siege of Cawnpore
First running of the Queen's Plate at Toronto's Carleton Track - North America's oldest continuously run horse race
-28] Battle at Garnett's/Golding's Farms, Virginia
Battle of Gaines's Mill, VA (Cold Harbor, Chickahominy Bluffs) Day 3
Bank of California opens doors
1st NLer to get 6 hits in 9 inn game (Dave Force, Philadelphia Athletics)
Democratic Party elects Samuel Tilden as US presidential candidate
Canadian boxer George Dixon becomes the first Black world champion when he stops English bantamweight champion Edwin "Nunc" Wallace in 18 rounds in London
Great stock crash on NY stock exchange
American Annie Londonderry [Annie Kopchovsky] sets out from Boston to become first woman to bicycle around the world (completes journey September 1895)

Industrial Workers of the World (IWW) is established at "The "Continental Congress of the Working Class" in Chicago, Illinois; members include representatives of the Socialist Labor Party/Socialist Trades & Labor Alliance, Socialist Party of America, Western Federation of Miners and the International Working People's Association
Russian sailors mutiny aboard the battleship "Potemkin" and sail for Odessa
The IEC (International Electrotechnical Commission) established in London with Lord Kelvin as President to standardize electrical units and terms
US signs treaty of commerce with Ethiopia
100°F (38°C), Fort Yukon, Alaska (state record)
Dutch SDAP demonstrates against conscription
Canadian hospital ship HMHS Llandovery Castle sunk by German U-boat off southern Ireland - only 24 of 258 people on board survive (U-boat officers later charged with war crimes)
Newberry Medal 1st presented for kids literature (Hendrik Van Loon)
First color television demonstration is performed by Bell Laboratories in NYC
German PresidentPaul von Hindenburgrefuses to pay the German reparation debt of WWI
Federal Savings & Loan Association created
Danno O'Mahoney beats Jim Londos in Boston, to become wrestling champ
1st night game at Cleveland Municipal Stadium (Indians 5, Tigers 0)
Brooklyn Dodgers tie Boston Bees, 2-2 in 23 innings; called on account of darkness after 5 hours 15 minutes
Soviet Army attacks Romania
Bialystok Poland falls to Germany
Nazi manifest against the Jews in Amsterdam
FBI captures 8 Nazi saboteurs from a sub off NY's Long Island
PQ-17 convoy leaves Iceland for Archangelsk
Elly Dammers throws Dutch record spear (41,43m)
Fanny Blankers-Koen runs Dutch record 200m (24.5)
Cherbourg, France liberated by Allies
Foundation 1940-45 established
98°F (36.8°C) in De Bilt, Netherlands
WRC TV channel 4 in Washington, D.C. (NBC) begins broadcasting
"Captain Video & His Video Rangers" debut on DUMONT-TV - Television's first sci-fi series
South Africa heeds United Nations call to assist Korea
US sends 35 military advisers to South Vietnam
Joseph Laniel appointed French premier
First nuclear power station opens in Obninsk near Moscow, Russia
Hungary beats Brazil, 4-2 in the "Battle of Berne" as FIFA World Cup quarter-final descends into an all-out brawl with 3 players sent-off; fighting continues in dressing rooms after final whistle
"Julius LaRosa Show" debuts on CBS-TV
First automobile seat belt legislation enacted, in Illinois
MLB Cleveland Indians trailing Baltimore Orioles 9-1, come back to win 12-11 in 11 innings
Hurricane Audrey, kills 526 in Louisiana & Texas
The British Medical Research Council publishes a report suggesting a direct link between smoking and lung cancer.
Billy Pierce's perfect game bid broken with 2 outs in 9th
Harry Burrell flies KC-135 from New York to London in record time of 5:27:42.8
British Somaliland becomes part of Somalia
Chlorophyll "A" synthesized in Cambridge, Massachusetts
Oil pipe line from Rotterdam-Ruhrgebied opens
Ghana imposes a total ban on exports to South Africa and South West Africa
Dutch soccer clubs Rapid JC and Roda Sports merge to form Roda JC Kerkrade; KNVB Cup winners 1996–97, 1999–2000

In South Africa, the General Law Amendment Act (Sabotage Act) No 76 commences, increasing the state president's power to declare organisations unlawful and to add further restrictions to banning orders
Billy J Kramer & Dakotas record Lennon-McCartney song "I Call Your Name"
MLB Philadelphia Phillies Johnny Callison hits for cycle, but teammate centerfielder Tony Gonzalez's error ends his record 205 consecutive errorless games
USAF Major Robert A Rushworth, flying in X-15 rocket powered aircraft, reaches altitude 285,000 feet (86,868 meters)
"Sie Liebt Dich (She Loves You)" by The Beatles, singing in German, peaks at #97
Dark Shadows, American Gothic soap opera, premieres on ABC-TV
Race riot in Buffalo NY (200 arrested)
World's first ATM is installed by Barclays Bank in Enfield, London
Ludvik Vaculik publishes "Manifest of 2000 words" in Prague
Honduras/El Salvador breaks diplomatic relations due to soccer match
Following the arrest of Bernadette Devlin, intense riots erupt in Derry and Belfast leading to a prolonged gun battle between Irish republicans and loyalists
Bill Graham's New York rock venue Fillmore East closes down, to be succeeded by Fillmore West in San Francisco
John Dean tells Watergate Committee about Nixon's "enemies list"
Uruguayan president Juan Maria Bordaberry dissolves parliament and heads a coup d'état - beginning of the civic-military dictatorship
American TV variety program "The Flip Wilson Show" last airs on NBC-TV, after 4 seasons, winning 2 Emmy Awards
US President Nixon visits USSR
Air France A-300B Airbus hijacked from Athens arrives at Entebbe, Uganda; four hijackers, members of Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine and Bader-Meinhof Gang in Germany
In South Africa, the National President of the Black People's Convention, Kenneth Hlaku Rachidi, declares that riots in Soweto have lead to a new era of political consciousness
Portuguese general Antonio Eanes elected president
5-4 Supreme Court decision allows lawyers to advertise
Djibouti (Afars & Issas) claims Independence from France
Henry Rono of Kenya sets record for 3,000 m, 7:32.1
Soyuz 30 carries 2 cosmonauts (1 Polish) to Salyut 6 space station
US Seasat 1, 1st oceanographic satellite, launched into polar orbit
Supreme Court rules employers may use quotas to help minorities
1st female state police graduates (NJ)
Dodgers' Jerry Reuss no-hits SF Giants 8-0
Italian plane crashes into Tyrrheense Sea, kills 81
US revives draft registration
Cambodia adopts its constitution
Pamela Jenks, aged 21, is crowned the 14th Miss Black America
4th NASA Space Shuttle Mission: Columbia 4 (STS-4) launches
Highest price paid for painting by a living artist 960,200 pounds - Joan Miro
Maxie Anderson and Don Ida, American balloonists, die during a crash landing in endurance race in Schweinfurt, West Germany
MLB Seattle Mariners bat out of order against Chicago White Sox in 2nd inning
NASA launches space vehicle S-205
Soyuz T-9 carries 2 cosmonauts to Salyut 7 space station
Fire destroys a set in "A View to a Kill"
Late Night's 1st Tower Drop
Supreme Court ends NCAA monopoly on college football telecasts
First hotel strike in New York City
Route 66 (Chicago to Santa Monica) is decommissioned
American tennis player Anne White shocks Wimbledon by wearing a white, one-piece, lycra body suit in 1st round match against Pam Shriver; wears regular outfit after rain break
In referendum, Irish uphold ban on divorce
Robby Thompson (SF Giants) sets record, caught stealing 4 times in 1 game
South African Journalist and founder of the 'New Nation' newspaper, Zwelakhe Sisulu is abducted; he was released 721 days later on 2 December 1988
US informs New Zealand it will no longer defend NZ against attack due to its nuclear-free policy
World Court rules US aid to Nicaraguan contras illegal
"The Living Daylights", 15th James Bond film, 1st film to star Timothy Dalton premieres in London
In South Africa, the Afrikaans Protestant Church, a breakaway faction of Dutch Reformed Church, is formed
Reds bat out of order against Padres in 1st inning
NBA Draft: Syracuse power forward Derrick Coleman first pick by New Jersey Nets
Dan O'Brien fails on pole vault & eliminated from Olympics decathlon
Daryl Gates retires as LA police chief
NY Met pitcherAnthony Youngloses record 24th straight game
118°F (47.8°C) at Lakewood, New Mexico a state record
Aerosmith is the first major band to let fans download a full new track for free from the internet
NY Daily News increases prices to 50 cents
Holland's debut in English domestic comp (v Northants, NatWest)
Mason City Iowa's TV news personality Jodi Huisentruit disappears
Space shuttle STS-71 (Atlantis 14), launches
William Alford, an engineer for rival radio station WMMS, is sentenced to 10 days in jail and fined $1,000 for cutting the feed during Howard Stern's live broadcast from Cleveland
Kuala Lumpur International Airport opens in Malaysia
NHL Draft: Rimouski Oceanic (QMJHL) center Vincent Lecavalier first pick by Tampa Bay Lightning
ATB go to No.1 on the UK singles chart with "9pm, Till I Come"
The Chemical Brothers go #1 on the UK album chart with 'Surrender', their second #1 album
International Court of Justice finds against the United States in the LaGrand Case
NBA Draft: Glynn Academy center Kwame Brown first pick by Washington Wizards
US National Do Not Call Registry is created by the Federal Trade Commission to combat unwanted telemarketing calls and enrolls almost three-quarters of a million phone numbers on its first day
"Bad Day" single is released by Daniel Powter, 1st song to sell 2 million digital copies in the US (Billboard Song of the Year 2006)
Brazil's Military Police invade the favelas of Complexo do Alemão, remembered as the Complexo do Alemão massacre
The Tower of Hercules in northern Spain - only preserved Roman lighthouse still in use is inscribed as a World Heritage Site
NBA Draft: UNLV power forward Anthony Bennett first pick Cleveland Cavaliers
Tsakhiagiin Elbegdorj is re-elected President of Mongolia
NHL Coyotes franchise changes geographic name from "Phoenix" to "Arizona" after change of ownership
US Supreme Court strikes down Texas law restricting abortion 5-3
African Development Bank president Akinwumi Adesina wins the World Food Prize
European Union fines Google record $2.7 billion for unfair competition practices
Petya malware cyber-attack affects organisations in more than 64 countries
Complex carbon-based molecules found by Cassini spacecraft on Saturn moon Enceladus, previously only ever found on earth and meteorites in research published in "Nature"
French government introduces plan to bring back national service for 16 year-olds
Japanese spacecraft Hayabusa 2 arrives at diamond-shaped asteroid Ryugu
President Salva Kiir of South Sudan and former Vice President Riek MacharSouth sign peace deal to end four-year civil war
Chief Apple designer Jony Ive, designer of the iMac and the iPhone, announces he is leaving Apple after 30 years
US Supreme Court rules 2020 census cannot contain a question about citizenship in a 5-4 ruling
US Supreme Court rules the Constitution doesn't prohibit partisan gerrymandering, allowing a ruling party to redraw electoral boundaries
Ireland's coalition government appoints Micheál Martin as Prime Minister after four months of negotiations
Locust invasion labelled 'Swarmageddon' by The Times of India as it reaches Delhi
Boat sinks off Tunisia, drowning 43 migrants with 84 rescued by Tunisian navy
Heat dome envelops the Pacific Northwest with Portland posting highest temperature since records began of 112 F (broken the next day)
Hottest temperature ever recorded in Canada at 46.6 C (116 F) in Lytton, British Columbia (breaks record 2 days later with 49.6 C)
51 migrants die after being found inside an abandoned truck in San Antonio, Texas in extreme heat, with 16 people hospitalized
First rocket launch in Australia for 25 years and NASA's first commercial spaceport outside the US for sub-orbital rocket from Arnhem Space Centre, Northern Territory
Russian missile strike on Ukrainian shopping center in Kremenchuk, with 1000 people inside kills at least 13
Archaeologists in Roman city of Pompeii uncover a "pizza" painting in a fresco from house next to a bakery (more likely flatbread as lacks essential ingredients tomatoes and mozzarella)
Austrian artist  Gustav Klimt's last completed portrait “Dame mit Fächer” (Lady with a Fan) sells for £85.3 million ($108.4 million) in London -  most expensive artwork sold at a European auction
French police officer shoots dead a 17-year old at a traffic stop in Paris, prompting a wave of violent protests across the country
US Supreme Court rejects the "independent state legislature” theory that would have given state legislatures power to set rules for federal elections and draw congressional maps
US Supreme Court rule 6-3 to limit lower courts' ability to issue nationwide injunctions in case involving executive order banning birthright citizenship
Juan Rodríguez Cabrillosets sail from the Mexican port of Navidad on the first European voyage to explore the west coast of North America for the Spanish Empire
Great Northern War:Peter the Greatof Russia defeatsCharles XIIof Sweden at the Battle of Poltava; Charles goes into exile in Bender, Ottoman Empire
Flora MacDonald helpsBonnie Prince Charlie, disguised as Betty Burke an Irish maid, evade capture by landing him on the Isle of Skye
British generalJames Wolfebegins the siege of Quebec.
French Revolution: KingLouis XVIorders the nobility and clergy of the Estates-General to meet with the Third Estate, by then called the National Assembly

James Donnellybecomes engaged in a drunken brawl with Patrick Farrell, who suffers a fatal blow to the head. Farrell dies two days later, which makes James Donnelly a wanted man and draws the Donnelly family into the notorious feud
US Civil War: Skirmish at Fairfax Courthouse, Virginia delays Confederate cavalry leader GeneralJ.E.B. Stuart's travel to Gettysburg
US Civil War: Atlanta Campaign - Battle of Kennesaw Mountain, Georgia: UnionGeneral Shermanmakes unsuccessful frontal attack on Confederate defense
Cecil Rhodes' colonists attack Motlousi in Matabeleland
Review ofPaul Laurence Dunbar's second book, "Majors and Minors" by William Dean Howells in "Harper's Weekly" brings the poet to national attention
Defending championJack Johnsonbeats fellow American Frank Moran on points in 20 rounds in Paris, France to retain his lineal heavyweight boxing title
Eleftherios Venizelostakes over as Prime Minister of Greece and severs relations with Central Powers, bringing Greece onside with the Allies in WWI
North Korean troops reach Seoul, causing the UN to ask member states to aid South Korea.Harry Trumanorders the US Air Force and Navy into the Korean conflict.
CIA-sponsored rebels overthrow the democratically elected government of Guatemala and force PresidentJacobo Árbenzto resign
Joseph Kasa-Vubuis sworn in as the 1st President of the Republic of the Congo after being elected by the two chambers of Parliament
NASA civilian pilotJoseph A. Walkertakes X-15 to 6,606 kph, 37,700 m
Ross Perotfounds Electronic Data Systems
US PresidentJohn F. Kennedyspend his 1st full day in Ireland

Denver Pop Festival opens; 50,000 attend;Frank Zappa,Creedence Clearwater Revival, andJimi Hendrixheadline; other performers include Three Dog Night, Tim Buckley, Big Mama Thornton, Johnny Winter,Joe Cocker, and Poco

Festival Express opens in Toronto, Canada - The Grateful Dead, The Band,Janis Joplin, Flying Burrito Bros, Buddy Guy, Great Speckled Bird, and Delaney & Bonnie & Friends cross Canada together by train, for 5 shows in 3 cities
Legendary video game and home computer Atari, Inc. founded byNolan Bushnelland Ted Dabney in Sunnyvale, California
"Live & Let Die", 8th James Bond Film, 1st to starRoger Moore, also starring Jane Seymour, 1st released in the US
Willie McCoveysmashes 2 HRs in inning for 2nd time (Apr 12, 1973)
HeavyweightMuhammad Aliconfirms that his 3rd retirement is final (it isn't)
"Bette DavisEyes" by Kim Carnes returns to #1 slot
Ali Khameneinarrowly escapes an assassination attempt by the Mujaheddin-e Khalq, when a bomb concealed in a tape recorder, explodes on a desk in front of him
Jim Henson's fantasy film "Labyrinth" written by Terry Jones, and starringDavid BowieandJennifer Connelly, is released in the US
Mike TysonKOs Michael Spink in 91 seconds, in Atlantic City ($67m)
JoseCansecosigns record $4,700,000 per year Oak A's contract
Salman Rushdie, condemned to death by Iran, contributes $8,600 to help earthquake victims in the country
"57 Channels (And Nothin' On)" single byBruce Springsteenpeaks at #68
Don Henley booed in Milwaukee when he dedicates the song "It's Not Easy Being Green" to PresidentBill Clinton
Tony Hawkis the first skateboarder to land a "900" (a 2½-revolution aerial spin) at the X Games in San Francisco, California
PopeJohn Paul IIbeatifies 28 Ukrainian Greek Catholics, including 27 martyrs mostly killed by the Soviet secret police, in Lviv, western Ukraine, during his first visit to the country
AMD files a broad antitrust complaint againstIntelin Federal District Court, alleging abuse of monopoly powers and antitrust violations
Gordon Brownbecomes Prime Minister of the United Kingdom
Bill Gatessteps down as Chairman of Microsoft to work full time for the Bill &Melinda GatesFoundation
Colombia's rebel group FARC officially end armed existence after 52 years in ceremony with PresidentJuan Manuel Santosin Mesetas
Mark Zuckerbergannounces Facebook has reached 2 billion monthly users
Tennis starSerena Williamsposes pregnant and nude for the cover of "Vanity Fair"
Democrat Representative Joseph Crowley is defeated in New York Primary election by liberal challengerAlexandria Ocasio-Cortez
Malaysian police announce seizure of goods linked to former prime ministerNajib Razak, worth $273 million, biggest in the country's history, including 567 handbags and 14 tiaras
US Supreme Court JusticeAnthony Kennedyannounces he will retire on July 31
Ryan Seacrestannounced as the new host of "Wheel of Fortune", to replace long-time hostPat Sajakin 2024
First debate between presumptive US presidential candidatesJoe BidenandDonald Trump, hosted by CNN, raises fears aboutBidenas he appears to stumble with his words
War of the Austrian Succession: KingGeorge IIof Britain personally leads Allied troops to victory in the Battle of Dettingen in Bavaria. The last time a British monarch commanded troops in the field.
Liberty Bell returns home to Philadelphia after the British departure
Canadian-American adventurer Joshua Slocum arrives in Newport, Rhode Island, completing the 1st solo circumnavigation of the globe
Capt. Lowell H. Smith and Lt. John P. Richter perform the first-ever aerial refueling in a DH-4B biplane
USSR returns to the Gregorian calendar, using Sunday as a rest day, after 6 years using a Russian six-day calendar
